Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails (via search_emails or list_emails) for Marcus Thorne's escalation about the Sunbelt and Palmetto invoice attachment swap (email_marcus_invoice_safeguard_escalation) and Elena Rostova's reply demanding a name and date by Monday (email_elena_safeguard_directive).
OE 2: Search Linear for all engineering team issues (linear_list_issues filtered by team_id = team_engineering) to get the full list of open tickets, including ExpenseBot remediation tickets (ENG-211 through ENG-220) and the invoice attachment safeguard ticket (ENG-187). Expected findings: cross-referencing against Slack and email (OEs 4–5) reveals that ENG-212, ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217, and ENG-218 are all completed or deployed per engineer communications but still show in_progress in Linear (part of the "completing work without updating tickets" pattern). ENG-220 is partially complete (Layer 1 done in staging, Layer 2 blocked by DOT work). ENG-187 (invoice safeguard) is in backlog with no assignee.
OE 3: Get the ExpenseBot project details from Linear (linear_get_project on proj_expense_autocategorizer) to check the overall project status.
OE 4: Search Slack messages (via conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on channel C003) in the #engineering channel for recent updates from Lena (ENG-217 deployed), Dmitri (ENG-212 done), Anh (ENG-218 live), and Dmitri's April 25 message that his DOT validation scripts are done and he is clear for new work.
OE 5: Search emails (via search_emails) sent to Samira Tariq for status updates: Lena's email confirming ENG-217 done and saying she cannot take the safeguard ticket due to DOT work, Anh's email confirming ENG-218 shipped and asking what to work on next, Dmitri's email with ENG-220 partial progress (Layer 1 done, Layer 2 blocked) and ENG-212 done, and Anh's April 25 email saying he is out sick with a fever and will not be working for the next few days. Expected findings on team bandwidth: Lena is blocked by DOT compliance work (per her email). Dmitri finished DOT validation scripts on April 25 (per his Slack message) and is free after the documentation write-up. Anh completed ENG-218 but is out sick and unavailable. Dmitri has the most bandwidth.
OE 6: Search calendar events (via calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to or calendar_search_events) for the engineering team next week to find existing time commitments and identify open slots for the backlog reset sync.
OE 7: Search Airtable stipend records (via airtable_search_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblStipends00001) for Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics to check if any still show incorrect approval statuses from the pilot config bugs (e.g., Maria Santos' $550 standing desk still shows Rejected, Andre Washington's $1,200 desk still shows Approved).
OE 8: Search QuickBooks invoices (via quickbooks_search_invoices) for Sunbelt and Palmetto to understand the financial details of the invoice swap incident that Marcus escalated.
OE 9: Assign the invoice safeguard ticket (linear_update_issue on linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 / ENG-187) to Dmitri Volkov (assignee_id: moveops_dmitri_volkov), since Anh is out sick and Lena deferred due to DOT compliance workload (per her email).
OE 10: Create a new Linear ticket (linear_create_issue) under team_engineering for implementing Elena's $100 manual review threshold for stipend approvals as a separate high-priority work item.
OE 11: Look up email addresses (via contacts_search_contacts) for Marcus Thorne, Elena Rostova, Lena Bjorkstrom, Dmitri Volkov, and Anh Nguyen.
OE 12: Send email (via send_email) from samira.tariq@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com, CC elena.rostova@moveops.com, acknowledging the safeguard gap, naming Dmitri as the assigned owner, providing a timeline, and including QuickBooks findings on the Sunbelt/Palmetto situation.
OE 13: Send email (via send_email) from samira.tariq@moveops.com to lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com asking her to update her open Linear tickets with the current status.
OE 14: Send email (via send_email) from samira.tariq@moveops.com to dmitri.volkov@moveops.com with three items: (1) update his open Linear tickets with current status, (2) provide a realistic completion date for duplicate detection (ENG-220), and (3) notify him of his new safeguard ticket assignment (ENG-187) and ask him to review the ticket comments before starting.
OE 15: Send email (via send_email) from samira.tariq@moveops.com to anh.nguyen@moveops.com acknowledging his sick leave and wishing him well, or adjusting the original Anh email content given that the safeguard assignment no longer applies to him.
OE 16: Schedule a 30-minute engineering sync (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for next week with attendees samira.tariq@moveops.com, lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, and anh.nguyen@moveops.com.
OE 17: Post a message (via conversations_add_message) in the #engineering Slack channel (channel C003) announcing the priority reset, naming the safeguard and $100 threshold as top items, and summarizing the status of each open workstream.
OE 18: Update the ExpenseBot project (via linear_update_project on proj_expense_autocategorizer) to reflect the current state.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 1: Search emails (via search_emails or list_emails) for Marcus Thorne's escalation about the Sunbelt and Palmetto invoice attachment swap (email_marcus_invoice_safeguard_escalation) and Elena Rostova's reply demanding a name and date by Monday (email_elena_safeguard_directive).
OE 2: Search Linear for all engineering team issues (linear_list_issues filtered by team_id = team_engineering) to get the full list of open tickets, including ExpenseBot remediation tickets (ENG-211 through ENG-220) and the invoice attachment safeguard ticket (ENG-187). Expected findings: cross-referencing against Slack and email (OEs 4–5) reveals that ENG-212, ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217, and ENG-218 are all completed or deployed per engineer communications but still show in_progress in Linear (part of the "completing work without updating tickets" pattern). ENG-220 is partially complete (Layer 1 done in staging, Layer 2 blocked by DOT work). ENG-187 (invoice safeguard) is in backlog with no assignee.
OE 3: Get the ExpenseBot project details from Linear (linear_get_project on proj_expense_autocategorizer) to check the overall project status.
OE 4: Search Slack messages (via conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on channel C003) in the #engineering channel for recent updates from Lena (ENG-217 deployed), Dmitri (ENG-212 done), Anh (ENG-218 live), and Dmitri's April 25 message that his DOT validation scripts are done and he is clear for new work.

Expected findings on team bandwidth: Lena is blocked by DOT compliance work (per her email). Dmitri finished DOT validation scripts on April 25 (per his Slack message) and is free after the documentation write-up. Anh completed ENG-218 but is out sick and unavailable. Dmitri has the most bandwidth.
OE 6: Search calendar events (via calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to or calendar_search_events) for the engineering team next week to find existing time commitments and identify open slots for the backlog reset sync.
OE 7: Search Airtable stipend records (via airtable_search_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblStipends00001) for Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics to check if any still show incorrect approval statuses from the pilot config bugs (e.g., Maria Santos' $550 standing desk still shows Rejected, Andre Washington's $1,200 desk still shows Approved).
OE 8: Search QuickBooks invoices (via quickbooks_search_invoices) for Sunbelt and Palmetto to understand the financial details of the invoice swap incident that Marcus escalated.
OE 9: Assign the invoice safeguard ticket (linear_update_issue on linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 / ENG-187) to Dmitri Volkov (assignee_id: moveops_dmitri_volkov), since Anh is out sick and Lena deferred due to DOT compliance workload (per her email).
OE 10: Create a new Linear ticket (linear_create_issue) under team_engineering for implementing Elena's $100 manual review threshold for stipend approvals as a separate high-priority work item.
OE 11: Look up email addresses (via contacts_search_contacts) for Marcus Thorne, Elena Rostova, Lena Bjorkstrom, Dmitri Volkov, and Anh Nguyen.

OE 13: Send email (via send_email) from samira.tariq@moveops.com to lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com asking her to update her open Linear tickets with the current status.
OE 14: Send email (via send_email) from samira.tariq@moveops.com to dmitri.volkov@moveops.com with three items: (1) update his open Linear tickets with current status, (2) provide a realistic completion date for duplicate detection (ENG-220), and (3) notify him of his new safeguard ticket assignment (ENG-187) and ask him to review the ticket comments before starting.
OE 15: Send email (via send_email) from samira.tariq@moveops.com to anh.nguyen@moveops.com acknowledging his sick leave and wishing him well, or adjusting the original Anh email content given that the safeguard assignment no longer applies to him.
OE 16: Schedule a 30-minute engineering sync (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for next week with attendees samira.tariq@moveops.com, lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, and anh.nguyen@moveops.com.

3 The email body sent to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (visible in the send_email content parameter) acknowledges the invoice attachment safeguard gap and references that this is a second or repeat pricing-exposure incident (e.g., mentioning the Terraform leak, second incident, or repeat failure mode).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body to Marcus explicitly references 'the second incident' and 'after the Terraform incident' multiple times (e.g., 'The second incident confirms that was the wrong tradeoff' and 'both Terraform and Sunbelt/Palmetto' and 'after the Terraform incident').
✓Run #2The email content explicitly mentions 'two incidents on the same failure mode' and references 'March 18: We opened ENG-187 after the Terraform pricing leak' — acknowledging both the safeguard gap and that this is a repeat/second incident after Terraform.
✓Run #3The email content says 'You're right that two incidents on the same unbuilt control is not defensible' and also explicitly mentions 'This is the second time we've had a pricing exposure from mismatched attachments (first was the Terraform incident in March)' in the Sunbelt/Palmetto section. The safeguard gap is clearly acknowledged.
✓Run #4The email to Marcus explicitly references 'A second incident just happened' and mentions the 'Terraform pricing leak' as the first incident context, and states 'This is exactly the failure mode ENG-187 was opened to prevent after Terraform.'
✓Run #5The email content says 'this is on engineering prioritization, and I take responsibility for leaving it dormant after Terraform' (references Terraform as prior incident) and the Slack post also references 'Two confirmed pricing-exposure incidents on the same unbuilt safeguard.' The email clearly acknowledges the safeguard gap and second incident.
✓Run #6The email content to Marcus explicitly says 'two incidents on the same unbuilt control' and references the Terraform pricing leak: 'It came out of the Terraform pricing leak cleanup.' It clearly acknowledges the safeguard gap and frames it as a second incident.
4 The email body sent to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (visible in the send_email content parameter) names Dmitri Volkov as the assigned owner of the invoice safeguard ticket.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✗✓✗ 33%
✓Run #1The email body to Marcus explicitly states 'ENG-187 is now assigned to Dmitri Volkov' and 'I've committed you to starting Monday April 27'.
✗Run #2The email to Marcus names Lena Björkström as the assigned owner, not Dmitri Volkov. The agent assigned ENG-187 to Lena despite Anh being out sick and Lena having deferred the ticket due to DOT compliance workload, whereas Dmitri had the most bandwidth after finishing his DOT work.
✗Run #3The email to Marcus names 'Anh Nguyen' as the assigned owner, not Dmitri Volkov. Anh was out sick per his April 25 email, making Dmitri the correct choice. The agent failed to account for Anh's sick leave when assigning the ticket.
✗Run #4The email to Marcus names Anh Nguyễn (not Dmitri Volkov) as the assigned owner of the safeguard ticket. The agent failed to detect that Anh was out sick (per his April 25 email to Samira) and incorrectly assessed Anh as having the most bandwidth.
✓Run #5The email to Marcus explicitly states 'Dmitri Volkov is now the named owner on ENG-187 (Invoice email template safety checks — prevent attachment mismatches).' Dmitri is correctly named as the assignee.
✗Run #6The email to Marcus names Anh Nguyễn as the assigned owner ('ENG-187 is now assigned to Anh Nguyễn'), not Dmitri Volkov. The rubric requires Dmitri because Anh is out sick. The agent did not find Anh's out-sick email and incorrectly assigned the ticket to Anh.
5 The email body sent to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (visible in the send_email content parameter) provides a specific timeline for when a basic pre-send validation check will be in place (e.g., a number of days or weeks, a target date, or a sprint reference).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Marcus provides specific dates: 'Realistic ship date for the stopgap: Friday May 1' and 'Full safeguard suite: target May 8'.
✓Run #2The email includes 'Target ship date for a basic pre-send validation check: May 4, 2026' — a specific target date satisfying the requirement for a concrete timeline.
✓Run #3The email to Marcus provides specific dates: 'Realistic ship date for a basic validation check (items 1-2 above as a minimum viable stopgap): Friday May 1. Full implementation including batch confirmation and audit logging: by end of following week (May 8).'
✓Run #4The email to Marcus provides specific dates: 'By end of next week (May 1): Basic validation check deployed' and 'Week of May 4: Full implementation.'
✓Run #5The email to Marcus provides specific dates: 'Ship date: Friday May 1 for the basic validation gate' and 'Full implementation...target May 8.' This is specific, not vague.
✓Run #6The email to Marcus provides a specific timeline: 'Anh is targeting a shippable stopgap within 1–2 weeks (by approximately May 8).' This is a concrete target date, not vague language.
6 The email body sent to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (visible in the send_email content parameter) mentions the specific clients affected by the invoice swap: Sunbelt Venture Partners receiving Palmetto Community Foundation's nonprofit-rate invoice, or Palmetto receiving Sunbelt's commercial invoice.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Marcus specifically states: 'Sunbelt received Palmetto's $2,400 nonprofit-rate invoice and Palmetto received Sunbelt's $4,800 commercial invoice', naming both clients and the directional swap with pricing details.
✓Run #2The email explicitly states 'Sunbelt received Palmetto's $2,400 nonprofit invoice PDF, and Palmetto received Sunbelt's $4,800 commercial invoice PDF' — clearly identifying both the directional swap and the pricing type (nonprofit vs. commercial).
✓Run #3The email to Marcus explicitly states: 'Sunbelt received the $2,400 nonprofit-rate PDF, and Palmetto received the $4,800 commercial-rate PDF' — the directional swap with specific pricing is included.
✓Run #4The email to Marcus explicitly states 'Sunbelt received Palmetto's $2,400 nonprofit invoice PDF, and Palmetto received Sunbelt's $4,800 commercial invoice PDF,' detailing the directional swap with pricing specifics.
✓Run #5The email states 'Sunbelt received the $2,400 Palmetto PDF, and Palmetto received the $4,800 Sunbelt PDF' — explicitly calling out the directional swap and the nonprofit vs. commercial pricing mismatch.
✓Run #6The email to Marcus explicitly states: 'Sunbelt received Palmetto's nonprofit-rate PDF, and Palmetto received Sunbelt's commercial-rate PDF.' This names both clients and specifies the directional swap with rate type details.

27 The email body sent to dmitri.volkov@moveops.com (visible in the send_email content parameter) also asks him to update his open Linear tickets with current status, since completed work is not reflected in the tracker.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Dmitri explicitly asks: 'ENG-212 (schema validation tests) — you confirmed this is done... Please mark it Done. ENG-216 (pilot audit) — the audit is complete and delivered. Please mark it Done.' This satisfies the ticket-update request in addition to the completion date ask.
✓Run #2The email to Dmitri explicitly asks: 'Your Linear tickets for ENG-212 (edge case coverage) and ENG-216 (full audit) still show as In Progress...Please mark both as Done.' This is a clear request to update his tickets.
✓Run #3Dmitri's email includes a dedicated 'LINEAR TICKET CLEANUP' section asking him to move ENG-216 and ENG-212 to Done, stating 'Your shipped work is still showing as In Progress in Linear.'
